name: Murmansk Oblast (Russia)

colors.symbolism: the seas of the Arctic and the polar sky
the courage of the Kola Peninsula's people and the historic banners of Russia

description: A blue field over a red base in 4:1 proportions, charged in the centre with a stylised golden aurora borealis radiating across the field.
history: Adopted on 6 June 2004 by the Murmansk Oblast Duma.

features.symbolism: blue stands for the Barents and White Seas and the Arctic sky; red for the courage of the people of the Kola Peninsula.
a stylised golden aurora borealis, the defining natural phenomenon of the Murmansk skies.
